description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, tilted array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a flat, rectangular platform. The platform appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and concrete, with a grid-like surface texture and several raised cylindrical structures, likely representing ventilation or cooling units. The solar panel array is supported by a robust frame and is oriented at an angle to maximize sunlight exposure. The installation is bordered by solid black walls on three sides, possibly for protection or structural support. The surface of the platform is mostly smooth with some patterned sections, and there are no visible electrical cables or inverter housings. The overall design suggests a fixed, ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban rooftops or dedicated solar fields.
description: A large, tilted solar panel array mounted on a flat, grid-textured platform with protective side walls and cylindrical ventilation units.
description_view_0: From this view, the tilted solar panel array and two cylindrical units on the platform are clearly visible, with the black side walls framing the installation.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the grid pattern on the platform, the alignment of the solar panels, and the arrangement of the cylindrical units.
description_view_2: The view from above shows the full extent of the solar panel array and the symmetrical layout of the platform and its features.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the tilt of the solar panels and the depth of the platform, with the side walls and surface details in clear view.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum) frame and platform base
attribute_array_configuration: single row of large panels arranged in a linear configuration
